title: Hello World
description: Write a program that prints 'Hello, World!' exactly (with the comma, exclamation mark, and a newline at the end).

title: Sum Two Numbers
description:
Read two integers from a single line of stdin and print their sum.

Example:
Input: 3 5
Output: 8

starter_code.python:
a, b = map(int, input().split())
# Your code here

title: FizzBuzz
description:
Print the numbers from 1 to N (read from stdin), but:
- For multiples of 3 print 'Fizz'
- For multiples of 5 print 'Buzz'
- For multiples of both print 'FizzBuzz'

Example:
Input: 5
Output:
1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz

1
2
Fizz
4
Buzz
Fizz

FizzBuzz

title: Reverse a String
description:
Read a single line from stdin and print it reversed.

Example:
Input: hello
Output: olleh

title: Palindrome Check
description:
Read a single word from stdin and print 'YES' if it is a palindrome, or 'NO' otherwise. Ignore case.

Example:
Input: Racecar
Output: YES

Input: hello
Output: NO

title: Fibonacci Sequence
description:
Read an integer N from stdin and print the first N Fibonacci numbers, one per line. The sequence starts: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, ...

Example:
Input: 6
Output:
0
1
1
2
3
5

title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers and a target value, find the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target. Print the two indices (0-based) in ascending order on a single line, separated by a space. Assume exactly one solution exists.

Input format:
- Line 1: N (number of elements)
- Line 2: N space-separated integers
- Line 3: target

Example:
Input:
4
2 7 11 15
9
Output: 0 1

starter_code.python:
n = int(input())
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
target = int(input())
# Your code here

title: Count Words
description:
Read a single line of text from stdin and print the number of words in it. Words are separated by one or more spaces.

Example:
Input: hello world foo
Output: 3
